mnemonics: OPEN CLASS (Nouns, Verbs, Adjectives, Adverbs) can have new words added to them, like an OPEN door. CLOSED CLASS (Pronouns, Prepositions, Conjunctions, Determiners) is a CLOSED group with a fixed set of members.
keyPoints: Words are the smallest meaningful units of language.
Words are categorized into classes (parts of speech) based on their grammatical function and meaning.
The two main categories are Open Classes (accept new words) and Closed Classes (fixed membership).
commonMistakes: Confusing a word's class (e.g., thinking 'run' is only a verb, when it can also be a noun).
Assuming word classes are the same across all languages, ignoring language-specific grammatical rules.
